problem_statement: Non-programmer business users increasingly rely on AI-generated code for data analysis but it is unclear whether they can detect the LLM's errors; the paper tests whether such end-users can serve as effective human oversight of AI-generated analyses.
magnitude_evidence: 18 of 26 marketing/sales professionals reported using tools like ChatGPT to analyze data daily or weekly (which often involves code generation)
insufficiency_evidence: Participants frequently failed to detect critical flaws that could compromise decision-making, many of which required no technical knowledge to recognize; 'none were consistently detected; some safety-critical issues were missed entirely by all participants.'
